questions.q: Prafulla Chandra Ray is widely honoured with which title?
options: Father of the Indian Space Programme
questions.explanation: Prafulla Chandra Ray is celebrated as the 'Father of Indian Chemistry' for pioneering modern chemical research and industry in India.

questions.q: Prafulla Chandra Ray was a scientist in which field?
questions.explanation: P. C. Ray was a chemist renowned for his research and for founding India's chemical industry.

questions.q: Which pharmaceutical and chemical company did Prafulla Chandra Ray found?
questions.explanation: Ray founded Bengal Chemicals & Pharmaceutical Works, regarded as India's first pharmaceutical company.

questions.q: Bengal Chemicals, founded by P. C. Ray, is often described as India's first what?
questions.explanation: Bengal Chemicals & Pharmaceutical Works is widely regarded as India's first pharmaceutical company.

questions.q: Which chemical compound is Prafulla Chandra Ray most famous for discovering?
questions.explanation: Ray's celebrated discovery was a stable compound of mercurous nitrite, announced in 1896.

questions.q: Prafulla Chandra Ray was born in which present-day country?
questions.explanation: Ray was born in 1861 in the village of Raruli-Katipara, in a region that is now part of Bangladesh.

questions.q: In which century was Prafulla Chandra Ray born?
questions.explanation: P. C. Ray was born in 1861, in the 19th century.

questions.q: P. C. Ray is remembered not only as a scientist but also as a pioneering Indian what?
questions.explanation: Besides his research, Ray was a pioneering industrialist who built India's indigenous chemical industry.

questions.q: In which Indian city did P. C. Ray establish Bengal Chemicals and spend most of his academic career?
questions.explanation: Ray worked and built his enterprises in Calcutta (now Kolkata), the centre of his career.

questions.q: P. C. Ray wrote an influential book on the history of which subject in India?
questions.explanation: Ray authored 'A History of Hindu Chemistry', tracing the chemical knowledge of ancient and medieval India.

questions.q: In which year did P. C. Ray announce his discovery of mercurous nitrite?
questions.explanation: Ray reported the new stable compound mercurous nitrite in 1896, the work for which he is best known.

questions.q: Where did P. C. Ray earn his doctorate (D.Sc.) in chemistry?
questions.explanation: Ray studied at the University of Edinburgh in Scotland, earning both his B.Sc. and D.Sc. there.

questions.q: P. C. Ray travelled to Britain for higher studies after winning which scholarship?
questions.explanation: Ray won the Gilchrist Prize Scholarship in 1882, which enabled him to study chemistry in Edinburgh.

questions.q: At which institution did P. C. Ray serve as a professor of chemistry for much of his career?
questions.explanation: Ray taught chemistry at Presidency College, Calcutta, mentoring a generation of Indian chemists.

questions.q: In what year did P. C. Ray found Bengal Chemicals & Pharmaceutical Works as a company?
questions.explanation: Ray formally incorporated Bengal Chemicals & Pharmaceutical Works in 1901, though he began the venture in the 1890s.

questions.q: The subtitle on Prafulla Chandra Ray's card calls him the 'Father of Indian Chemistry.' Which chemical class did mercurous nitrite open up for study?
options: Stable salts of mercurous compounds with nitrite
questions.explanation: Ray's discovery showed a stable mercurous (mercury(I)) salt of nitrous acid could be isolated, prompting wider study of mercury and ammonium nitrite chemistry.

questions.q: Which renowned Indian scientist was a student and close associate of P. C. Ray at Presidency College?
questions.explanation: The astrophysicist Meghnad Saha was among the brilliant students mentored by P. C. Ray at Presidency College.

questions.q: P. C. Ray's research school is especially associated with the chemistry of which metals and their salts?
questions.explanation: Following his mercurous nitrite work, Ray and his students extensively studied nitrites of mercury and other heavy metals.

questions.q: What was a major motivation behind P. C. Ray founding an Indian chemical industry?
options: To promote Indian self-reliance and create jobs (Swadeshi)
questions.explanation: Ray was a champion of the Swadeshi ideal, building indigenous industry to foster Indian self-reliance and employment.

questions.q: P. C. Ray wrote a notable autobiography. What was it titled?
options: Life and Experiences of a Bengali Chemist
questions.explanation: Ray's autobiography, 'Life and Experiences of a Bengali Chemist', records his scientific and entrepreneurial journey.

questions.q: Which professional society in India was P. C. Ray instrumental in founding to promote chemistry?
questions.explanation: Ray was a founder and the first president of the Indian Chemical Society, established in 1924.

questions.q: In what approximate decade did P. C. Ray die?
questions.explanation: P. C. Ray died in 1944, in the 1940s, at the age of 82.

questions.q: P. C. Ray's doctoral thesis at Edinburgh dealt with which family of compounds?
options: Conjugated sulphates of the copper-magnesium group
questions.explanation: His D.Sc. thesis was titled 'Conjugated Sulphates of the Copper-Magnesium Group: A Study of Isomorphous Mixtures and Molecular Combinations'.

questions.q: In which scientific journal did P. C. Ray first publish his discovery of mercurous nitrite in 1896?
options: Journal of the Asiatic Society of Bengal
questions.explanation: Ray announced the new compound in the Journal of the Asiatic Society of Bengal in 1896.

questions.q: Which year roughly marks the small-scale start of P. C. Ray's chemical venture, before Bengal Chemicals was formally incorporated?
questions.explanation: Ray began producing chemicals on a small scale around 1892 in his Calcutta home, years before formal incorporation in 1901.

questions.q: P. C. Ray's 'A History of Hindu Chemistry' notably examined and translated material from which ancient and medieval tradition?
questions.explanation: The work surveyed Indian rasashastra (alchemy and iatrochemistry) drawn from Sanskrit chemical and medical texts.

questions.q: P. C. Ray was a Fellow of which prestigious British scientific society, reflecting his international standing?
options: Chemical Society of London (later Royal Society of Chemistry)
questions.explanation: Ray was honoured as a Fellow of the Chemical Society of London, a mark of his recognition by the international chemistry community.

questions.q: Which British honour was conferred on P. C. Ray in recognition of his scientific contributions?
options: Companion of the Order of the Indian Empire (CIE), and later a knighthood
questions.explanation: Ray was made a Companion of the Order of the Indian Empire (CIE) in 1912, and was later knighted in 1919, in recognition of his work.

questions.q: What was distinctive about the stability of mercurous nitrite that made Ray's 1896 discovery significant?
options: It was the first stable salt of mercury(I) with the nitrite ion to be isolated
It was the first organic compound made in India
questions.explanation: The significance lay in isolating a stable mercurous (mercury(I)) nitrite, a salt not previously obtained in pure crystalline form.

questions.q: After retiring from Presidency College, P. C. Ray continued teaching and research as Palit Professor of Chemistry at which institution?
options: University of Calcutta (University College of Science)
questions.explanation: Ray became the first Palit Professor of Chemistry at the University College of Science, University of Calcutta (the Rajabazar Science College).
